Description: The primary key uniquely identifies each transaction record.
The exact calendar date timestamp detailing when the transaction occurred.
Financial accounting period tracking monthly data continuity across fiscal years.
The classification of the account holder’s financial profile, indicating the primary source of income or the nature of expenses being tracked.
The expense classification grouping transactions into fund allocation.

Allowable Values: Association Dues', 'Cafe', 'Checkup', 'Cinema', 'Clothing', 'Dental', 'Dorm/Apartment Rent', 'Electricity', 'Emergency Fund', 'Fast Food', 'Fuel', 'Gadgets/Accessories', 'Gaming', 'Gifts/Donations', 'Groceries', 'Insurance', 'Internet', 'Investment', 'Jeepney/Bus Fare', 'Medicine', 'Miscellaneous', 'Mobile Load', 'Online Course', 'Online Shopping', 'Parking/Toll', 'Personal Care', 'Printing', 'Repair/Maintenance', 'Restaurant', 'Ride Hailing', 'School Supplies', 'Streaming Subscription', 'Travel/Leisure', 'Tuition/Fees', 'Water'
Description: The granular classification that breaks down each category into specific spending types.
The name of the business, vendor, or the service provider, identifying where or to whom the payment was made.

Allowable Values: Real numbers (≥ 0.00, Except valid negative values for refunds)
Description: The monetary value of the transaction in Philippine peso, including negative values for refunded transactions.
The channel is utilized to settle transactions or the financial expense.
The behavioral classification that tags each transaction based on its financial priority.
The current processing state of the transaction.
The maximum allocated spending threshold in Philippine Peso assigned for the corresponding spending category.
An optional free-text field for supplementary remarks about the transaction.
